title: Snyk Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Snyk** is a developer-first security platform that scans source, dependencies, container images, and IaC for vulnerabilities and license risk. It exposes two HTTP surfaces:

- **REST v3** at `https://api.snyk.io/rest` â€” JSON:API, requires `?version=YYYY-MM-DD~beta` (or a GA date) on every request, content type `application/vnd.api+json`.
- **Legacy v1** at `https://api.snyk.io/v1` â€” plain JSON. Used for surfaces not yet ported to v3 (`/user/me`, dependencies, org members, per-user notification settings).

This connector â€” `SnykConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "snyk"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) â€” wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from Snyk:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| User (self) | `/v1/user/me` | Identify the token holder; used as the health probe |
| Organizations | `/rest/orgs` | List + read orgs visible to the token |
| Projects | `/rest/orgs/{id}/projects` | List + read + delete scanned projects |
| Issues | `/rest/orgs/{id}/issues` | List + read open vulnerability / license issues |
| Targets | `/rest/orgs/{id}/targets` | List + read scan targets (repo / image / SBOM source) |
| Dependencies | `/v1/org/{id}/dependencies` | Legacy dependency graph search |
| Users | `/v1/org/{id}/members` | List org members |
| User settings | `/v1/user/me/notification-settings/org/{id}` | Per-org notification preferences for the token holder |

The connector normalises projects + issues into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces every user-requested operation as a standalone `async def` (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC â€” all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::SnykHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Snyk API token

1. Sign in to https://app.snyk.io.
2. Click your avatar â†’ **Account Settings â†’ General â†’ Auth Token**.
3. Click **Click to show** to reveal the token, then **Copy**.
4. (Optional) Note your default organization's UUID from **Settings â†’ General â†’ Organization ID**.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP â†’ Manage Connectors â†’ Snyk**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_token` (secret, required) | The token from step 3 |
| `default_org_id` (text, optional) | Org UUID used by `sync()` and shorthand calls |
| `api_version` (text, optional) | REST v3 version date, default `2024-10-15` |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.snyk.io/rest` |
| `v1_base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.snyk.io/v1` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, optional) | Soft cap, default 200 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_token` is present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `SnykHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` next. That probes `GET https://api.snyk.io/v1/user/me` with `Authorization: token <api_token>`. A 2xx â†’ `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. 401 / 403 â†’ `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. 429 â†’ `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

### 4. First call

List your organizations:

```python
result = await connector.list_organizations(limit=10)
for org in result["data"]:
    print(org["id"], org["attributes"]["name"])
```

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Snyk uses **API token authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Snyk-specific gotcha â€” "token" prefix, NOT "Bearer"

The Authorization header uses the literal `token` prefix on **every** request (both REST v3 and legacy v1):

```
Authorization: token <api_token>
```

This is the single most common Snyk integration error. If you send `Bearer <api_token>` the API responds 401 even with a valid token.

### Credential model

- `api_token` (required) â€” Snyk personal or service-account token. Held in `self.config` and applied by `SnykHTTPClient._rest_headers()` / `_v1_headers()` on every outbound call.
- `default_org_id` (optional) â€” the org slug or UUID `sync()` and `health_check()` fall back to.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_token` is non-empty. Persists install config. Does **not** call any Snyk endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the configured `api_token` (compatibility shim â€” no token exchange happens). |
| `health_check()` | `GET /v1/user/me`. Maps 401/403/429 via `_STATUS_MAP`. |
| `ensure_token()` / refresh | N/A â€” Snyk tokens never expire automatically; rotate by re-running install with a new value. |

### Header contract

```
# REST v3 (/rest/*)
Authorization: token <api_token>
Content-Type:  application/vnd.api+json
Accept:        application/vnd.api+json

# Legacy v1 (/v1/*)
Authorization: token <api_token>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` â€” never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_token` | secret | yes | `self.api_token` | Bearer-replacement: `Authorization: token <â€¦>` |
| `default_org_id` | text | no | `self.default_org_id` | Default org for `sync()` and shorthand calls |
| `api_version` | text | no | `self.api_version` (default `2024-10-15`) | REST v3 `?version=` param |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (default `https://api.snyk.io/rest`) | REST v3 base override |
| `v1_base_url` | text | no | `self.v1_base_url` (default `https://api.snyk.io/v1`) | Legacy v1 base override |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 200) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
CONNECTOR_TYPE = "snyk"
AUTH_TYPE      = "api_key"
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_REST_BASE       = "https://api.snyk.io/rest"
_V1_BASE         = "https://api.snyk.io/v1"
_DEFAULT_VERSION = "2024-10-15"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `SnykConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::SnykHTTPClient`. OCP: every user-named operation is a standalone `async def`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_token`; initialise `SnykHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth â€” returns a TokenInfo wrapping the configured API token.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Fetches the token holder via `GET /v1/user/me`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates projects + issues for `default_org_id` â†’ `NormalizedDocument` â†’ `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Organizations
children.content:
### `async list_organizations(version=None, limit=100, starting_after=None) -> Dict`
`GET /rest/orgs?version=â€¦&limit=â€¦[&starting_after=â€¦]`.

### `async get_organization(org_id) -> Dict`
`GET /rest/orgs/{org_id}`.

children.title: Projects
children.content:
### `async list_projects(org_id, *, target_id=None, types=None, limit=100, starting_after=None) -> Dict`
`GET /rest/orgs/{org_id}/projects`. `types` is a list; the client joins it as a CSV.

### `async get_project(org_id, project_id) -> Dict`
`GET /rest/orgs/{org_id}/projects/{project_id}`.

### `async delete_project(org_id, project_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /rest/orgs/{org_id}/projects/{project_id}`. Returns `{}` on 204.

children.title: Issues
children.content:
### `async list_issues(org_id, *, project_id=None, severity=None, type=None, limit=50, starting_after=None) -> Dict`
`GET /rest/orgs/{org_id}/issues`. `severity` is a list; the client joins it as a CSV.

### `async get_issue(org_id, issue_id) -> Dict`
`GET /rest/orgs/{org_id}/issues/{issue_id}`.

children.title: Targets
children.content:
### `async list_targets(org_id, *, source=None, limit=100, starting_after=None) -> Dict`
`GET /rest/orgs/{org_id}/targets`.

### `async get_target(org_id, target_id) -> Dict`
`GET /rest/orgs/{org_id}/targets/{target_id}`.

children.title: Dependencies Â· Users Â· Settings (legacy v1)
children.content:
### `async list_dependencies(org_id, *, project_id=None, limit=50) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/org/{org_id}/dependencies` â€” body: `{"filters": {"projects": [...]}}`.

### `async list_users(org_id) -> List`
`GET /v1/org/{org_id}/members` â€” returns a JSON array.

### `async list_org_members(org_id) -> List`
Alias for `list_users` â€” same endpoint.

### `async get_user_settings(org_id) -> Dict`
`GET /v1/user/me/notification-settings/org/{org_id}` â€” per-org notification preferences for the token holder.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
SnykError                              # base; carries status_code + response_body
â”śâ”€â”€ SnykAuthError                      # 401 / 403
â”śâ”€â”€ SnykBadRequestError                # 400
â”śâ”€â”€ SnykNotFoundError                  # 404
â”śâ”€â”€ SnykConflictError                  # 409
â”śâ”€â”€ SnykRateLimitError                 # 429 â€” retry_after_s
â””â”€â”€ SnykServerError                    # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
SnykNetworkError = SnykServerError      # legacy name from older callers
SnykNotFound     = SnykNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After` header; otherwise exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt`. Retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3`. |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `SnykServerError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 â†’ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 â†’ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)
429 â†’ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
net â†’ ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,  CONNECTED)
other â†’ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

`sync()` returns `SyncResult(status=FAILED, message=â€¦)` on any uncaught exception; per-document failures bump `documents_failed` and the final status becomes `PARTIAL` if any are non-zero.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_token` blank | Re-paste the token in ACP. The token is sealed at rest. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 from Snyk | Token was revoked or copied incorrectly. Generate a new one at Account Settings â†’ General â†’ Auth Token. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` for a freshly issued token | Sending `Authorization: Bearer <â€¦>` instead of `token <â€¦>` | Already fixed in `client/http_client.py::_rest_headers/_v1_headers`. If you patched these, restore the literal `token` prefix. |
| `snyk.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Snyk per-token rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff honouring `Retry-After`. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on `list_organizations` | Wrong `base_url` (typo in install field) | Reset `base_url` to `https://api.snyk.io/rest`. |
| 404 on `list_dependencies` | Wrong `v1_base_url` | Reset `v1_base_url` to `https://api.snyk.io/v1`. |
| `sync()` returns FAILED with "default_org_id is required" | Connector installed without an org context | Fill in `default_org_id` in ACP and re-install. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'SnykNetworkError'` | Older callers expect the legacy alias | Already fixed â€” `SnykNetworkError = SnykServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
| REST request missing `?version=` | Direct `httpx` call bypassing `SnykHTTPClient._rest_params()` | Always go through the client; `connector.py` never calls httpx directly. |
